mechanism: Universal fertility decline reduces working-age population growth, reversing the 1990-2015 global labor supply doubling and creating structural tightness in labor markets
regime_dependence: Holds universally in aging OECD economies; partially offset in countries with sustained high immigration; AI substitution timeline is the key uncertainty

mechanism: Labor scarcity drives wage pressure, shifts consumption toward labor-intensive non-tradeable services, and compresses corporate margins, creating supply-side inflationary pressure
regime_dependence: Depends on central bank credibility and fiscal policy stance; Japan was 27-year exception due to unique non-demographic factors; offset if AI substitution is rapid

mechanism: Demographic inflationary pressure shifts the economy from demand-shock dominance to supply-shock dominance, supporting positive stock-bond correlation
regime_dependence: Holds when demographics is a dominant inflation driver; overwhelmed during periods of monetary credibility restoration or demand-driven recessions

mechanism: Rising dependency ratios shift median voter from net taxpayer to net transfer recipient, creating democratic pressure for fiscal expansion that reinforces structural fiscal deterioration
regime_dependence: Stronger in democracies with older median voters; partially constrained by constitutional fiscal rules (Germany exception); less relevant in authoritarian systems

mechanism: Political economy pressure for fiscal expansion works against deleveraging while lower labor force growth caps the growth channel for debt reduction, creating a structural trap
regime_dependence: Most binding in high-debt countries with aging populations and democratic governance; less relevant for countries with young populations or fiscal rule frameworks

mechanism: Pension demand compresses term premia and lowers long rates, while fiscal supply from aging-related spending expands term premia — net r* effect depends on which force dominates at each maturity
regime_dependence: Forces reinforced each other 2000-2020 but now diverge; outcome depends on relative magnitude at different points on the yield curve, particularly 10Y-30Y

mechanism: Immigration restriction removes the primary short-term offset to demographic labor supply contraction, accelerating structural scarcity across sectors with high immigrant labor concentration
regime_dependence: Depends on whether OECD restriction is cyclical (18-36 months) or structural (10+ years); transmission varies by sector based on immigrant labor concentration (agricultural 50-70% foreign-born)

mechanism: Tight labor markets eliminate the traditional margin relief valve (headcount reduction) for leveraged borrowers facing refinancing, creating a novel dual squeeze on interest coverage
regime_dependence: Most acute during 2026-2028 maturity wall for leveraged borrowers in labor-intensive sectors; less relevant for capital-light businesses (software, financial services)

mechanism: EBITDA addbacks premised on labor rationalization synergies become unrealizable when tight labor markets prevent headcount reductions, amplifying credit quality deterioration at refinancing
regime_dependence: Applies in covenant-lite structures (~87% of issuance) where lenders cannot intervene; sector-dependent on labor intensity of the borrower

mechanism: Countries with high food CPI weights and growing populations experience amplified inflation from food price shocks, while rising import dependency exposes them to global supply shocks — creating a self-reinforcing instability loop
regime_dependence: Structural for Sub-Saharan Africa and high-population-growth EM countries; less relevant for aging developed economies with low food CPI weights

mechanism: China's fundamentally different structural conditions at aging onset (lower GDP per capita, weaker pension funding, negative NIIP trajectory) demonstrate Japan's non-generalizability and suggest outcomes may be worse than Japan's stagnation
regime_dependence: Structural comparison is factual; specific outcome path is genuinely uncertain across scenario weights (60/25/10/5)

mechanism: Income growth kills fertility (self-destructing call option), meaning the EM demographic dividend unwinds precisely as it materializes once countries cross the $8K-12K GDP per capita threshold
regime_dependence: Triggered at $8K-12K GDP per capita; speed depends on urbanization rate, female education, and access to contraception

mechanism: Political economy of aging drives increased government borrowing (fiscal supply) that collides with pension demand for duration, creating a reflexive loop where pension funds need the long bonds governments issue to fund pensions
regime_dependence: Most acute in countries with large DB pension sectors and rising dependency ratios; moderated by constitutional fiscal constraints

mechanism: If demographics constrains growth-based deleveraging, the policy response (financial repression, negative real rates) creates feedback to r* that is endogenous to the demographic trajectory
regime_dependence: Holds when fiscal dominance binds and central bank independence is compromised; less relevant if productivity growth substitutes for demographic growth

mechanism: Concurrent aging across all major economies eliminates cross-country demographic arbitrage that historically moderated the aggregate impact on global r*, potentially amplifying the effect
regime_dependence: Unprecedented condition with no historical template; effect depends on whether SSA and South Asia can partially offset as capital destinations

mechanism: Immigration restriction reduces agricultural labor supply (50-70% foreign-born), raising food production costs, which transmits differently across countries based on food CPI weights
regime_dependence: Strongest in countries dependent on immigrant agricultural labor with low mechanization alternatives; specialty crops most exposed

mechanism: AI could either substitute for scarce labor (reducing scarcity) or complement human labor (amplifying scarcity by increasing productivity of the scarce factor); resolution determines whether demographic headwinds are temporary or permanent
regime_dependence: 5-15 year transition period where both forces coexist; outcome depends on whether AI proves substitutive or complementary at macro scale

mechanism: Immigration restriction reduces household formation, compressing securitization pipeline (RMBS, consumer ABS) and reducing the supply of duration assets available to pension funds
regime_dependence: Relevant primarily in US and other countries with large securitization markets; depends on duration and severity of immigration restriction

mechanism: Aging farmer retirement and insufficient replacement constricts agricultural supply, creating food price pressure that is arithmetically amplified in countries with high food CPI weights and growing populations
regime_dependence: Binding for specialty crops in the near term; row crops offset by technology and capital; amplified in high-food-CPI-weight countries with population growth
